mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary policy that reduces borrowing costs and boosts equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in unemployment rate and increase in payrolls indicates strong labor market health, driving corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative when both UNRATE < 4.5% and PAYEMS > 100k are true, or pairwise correlation between proxies and returns drops below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A rising industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing activity, increasing corporate profits and generating positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative despite INDPRO growth > 0.5% in 3 months or INDPRO's predictive power (Rank IC) falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in real GDP signals robust economic growth, driving investor confidence and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative when GDPC1 shows quarterly growth or GDPC1's Rank IC drops below 0.07 over 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in residential construction and consumer spending indicates strong demand-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative when both HOUST > 1600 and RSAFS > 600k are true, or interaction term significance drops below 1.96 in regression models.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of high inflation and rising interest rates creates a 'stagflation' risk, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive when both CPIAUCSL > 275 and FEDFUNDS > 4.5% are true, or interaction term coefficient becomes non-significant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive despite UNRATE increases > 0.2pp in 3 months or UNRATE's predictive power (Rank IC) falls below 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of high inflation and low interest rates creates a 'growth-at-risk' scenario, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive when both CPIAUCSL > 270 and FEDFUNDS < 0.1% are true, or interaction term significance drops below 1.64 in regression models.
